title: OMR batch scoring: score candidate responses against the approved answer key (wire scoreResponses to real data)
business_reason: SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP evaluation_ops: 'scoring engine orphaned'. server/eval/scoring.ts (scoreResponses) exists and /api/staff/evaluation/score is a STATELESS scorer (computes from inline POST data, persists nothing). The real path — read an import batch's evaluation_candidate_responses + the approved answer key, score each candidate, and persist evaluation_candidate_scores — was unbuilt (and was blocked by the bad UNIQUE constraints fixed in FR-SCHEMA-UNIQUES-0007). This produces the scores that results ranking (FR-RESULTS-RANKING-0006) consumes.

design.engine: server/eval/scoreBatch.ts scoreBatch({actor, scoreBatchId}): load evaluation_score_batches -> import_batch_id + answer_key_id; require key_status='approved'; parse answer_key_payload (AnswerKey shape or flat map); load evaluation_candidate_responses for the import batch; scoreResponses(response_payload, key) each; UPSERT evaluation_candidate_scores (score_batch_id, candidate_id, raw_score, correct/wrong/blank counts) on the natural key fixed in 0023; set score_batch_status='scored' + scored_count; audited.
design.seed: seed_chain3.sql: E2E-AKEY-7002 gets an approved real answer_key_payload + E2ESCORE-1/2/3 responses (all-correct / partial / all-wrong).

param_14.question: How are candidate OMR responses scored against the answer key and persisted?
param_14.answer: A score-batch scoring run reads the linked import batch's candidate responses + the approved answer key, computes each candidate's score server-side (correct - wrong*penalty, with blanks), and upserts evaluation_candidate_scores keyed by (score_batch_id, candidate_id). The answer key must be approved. Scores are never client-supplied; the stateless /score endpoint remains for ad-hoc computation.
param_14.answer_source: evaluation_ops/workflows.json (score_batch_lifecycle), server/eval/scoring.ts, evaluation_candidate_responses/_scores schema, FR-SCHEMA-UNIQUES-0007 (enabled multi-candidate)
param_14.security_implications: scoring requires an APPROVED answer key (fail-closed); scores are server-computed, never client input; raw responses + scores are restricted staff data; scoring is audited; upsert is idempotent (re-score replaces, no double-count).
param_14.approval_implications: answer key must be approved before scoring; score-batch downstream approve_for_results stays dual-approval.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: evaluation_candidate_scores feed result generation -> candidate_results.raw_score, which results_ops:generate ranks (FR-0006). The score->result handoff (copy scores into candidate_results) is a follow-up.
param_14.assumptions: answer_key_payload is the AnswerKey shape ({answers, marksCorrect, marksWrong}) or a flat {qid: correct} map; responses live in evaluation_candidate_responses.response_payload; multi-candidate-per-batch enabled by 0023.
